milestone: A2 (A-b): multi-leaf APFS file-system tree (internal root over leaf nodes)
status: Passed (host reader lists every file + Apple kernel auto-mounts read-write + Apple fsck_apfs full clean on the multi-leaf fsroot tree). Multi-leaf fs-tree certified.
capability: buildFsTreeNodes splits the file-system tree into an internal root (level 1, oid 1028, keeps the btree_info trailer) over N leaf nodes (level 0, virtual oids consecutive from nx_next_oid, mapped per-node by the volume object map) once the records overflow a single node. Wired into every in-place commit (insert/delete/rename) via the shared finalizeFsCommit engine: the net block change (extra fs-tree nodes plus data) threads through the volume allocated-count, the CIB/spaceman free counts, and nx_next_oid. Lifts the ~15-root-file single-leaf cap to thousands.
host_verify: format-image 64 MiB volume MLEAF -> 20 chained commit-image-file-insert (leaf1..leaf20.txt); list-image reads all 20 files (no truncation); the fs-tree is a two-level tree (internal root + leaves). Unit test apfsWriter_inPlaceFileInsertGrowsIntoMultiLeafFsTree: chained inserts grow past the single-leaf cap, reader lists every file until the checkpoint data ring fills.

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; 20-file multi-leaf container (volume MLEAF) attached raw as SATA disk0 (bare APFS container, 67.1 MB, no GPT)
method: 1. format-image 64 MiB MLEAF -> 20x commit-image-file-insert leafN.txt (multi-leaf fsroot tree).
2. Apple kernel auto-synthesized + auto-mounted the container read-write (fsck banner: 'last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1)').
3. diskutil unmountDisk force disk1; fsck_apfs -n /dev/rdisk0 -> walked the multi-leaf fsroot tree, extent-ref tree, object maps, allocated space - all consistent.
vm_external_gate.kernel_behavior: macOS kernel auto-mounted the multi-leaf MLEAF volume read-write (apfs_kext touched the volume superblock on mount).
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: Checking the container superblock / checkpoint (xid) / space manager / object map; volume MLEAF formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer; Checking the fsroot tree / extent ref tree / volume object map space / allocated space; The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 (UUID 6C00A3D5-F0E4-45AB-9FF6-CB9B0DD97B5E) appears to be OK; The container /dev/rdisk0 appears to be OK.
vm_external_gate.multileaf_delete: Also certified an in-place DELETE on a multi-leaf tree (delete leaf10.txt from the 20-file container -> 19 files, still a two-level tree: internal root oid 1028 level 1 over leaf nodes 1042/1043). The macOS kernel auto-mounted it and `ls /Volumes/MLEAF` listed exactly the 19 remaining files (leaf10.txt absent); diskutil unmount; fsck_apfs -n full clean. Evidence: apfs-a2-multileaf-delete-fsck-PASS.png.
vm_external_gate.multileaf_rename: Also certified an in-place RENAME on a multi-leaf tree (leaf5.txt -> renamed5.txt, 20 files, still two-level: internal root oid 1028 over leaf nodes 1042/1043). The macOS kernel auto-mounted it and `ls /Volumes/MLEAF` showed 20 files with leaf5.txt gone and renamed5.txt present; fsck_apfs -n full clean. Evidence: apfs-a2-multileaf-rename-fsck-PASS.png. The full mutation set (insert + delete + rename) is Apple-certified on multi-leaf trees.
vm_external_gate.on_disk_split_verified: Byte inspection of the generated container confirms the genuine two-level split (not a single leaf): live fs-tree = internal root (oid 1028, otype 0x02 BTREE, flags 0x01 ROOT, level 1, 2 child records) over leaf nodes (otype 0x03 BTREE_NODE, flags 0x02 LEAF, level 0). 20 empty files = 64 fs-records (inode + dirent + dstream_id each, + base) overflowed one node.

fsck_found_defects: Leaf (non-root) fs-tree nodes were typed OBJECT_TYPE_BTREE (0x2, the root's type); a B-tree leaf must be OBJECT_TYPE_BTREE_NODE (0x3). fsck read the one-bit difference as a header bit flip and rejected the fsroot tree. Fixed: newFsTreeNode types a node by its ROOT flag.

The internal root's btree_info longest-key/value described only the root's 8-byte child-oid pointers (bt_longest_val 8) instead of the whole tree's largest record (160-byte inode). Fixed: writeFsTreeInfoTrailer measures over the full fs-record set for the internal root.
Both defects were accepted by the host reader and only surfaced under Apple fsck_apfs - exactly the class of bug the external validator exists to catch.
gates: clang-format + lizard + cppcheck clean; commits 60f9101 (wiring), 34c3086 (fsck fixes) - all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass. 121/121 unit tests pass (single-leaf regression clean).
next: Free-queue crash-safety implementation, fragmented/multi-extent files, arbitrary real-Apple containers, then the A-b matrix flip (physical-USB destructive + crash + rollback).
